title: Admin Recovery Patch: /admin/upgrade route + upgradeCurrentUserToAdmin backend function

requirements.text: Add a new backend function `upgradeCurrentUserToAdmin(code : Text)` to the existing main actor. If `code == "NAMMA5600"`, overwrite the caller's stored role to `#admin` (or the equivalent AppRole variant used in the existing schema) in the existing user profile storage (the same HashMap/storage used by the current role getter/checker). Return a success result. If the code does not match, return an error result with message "Invalid admin code". This function must overwrite any existing role (customer, driver, or admin) without restriction.

quotes: If code == "NAMMA5600": Overwrite caller's role to "admin" — Persist role in storage — Return success
This function must allow upgrading even if current role is "customer" or "driver". It must overwrite existing role.

acceptanceCriteria: Calling `upgradeCurrentUserToAdmin("NAMMA5600")` with any authenticated principal overwrites that principal's role to admin in persistent storage and returns a success result.
Calling `upgradeCurrentUserToAdmin("WRONGCODE")` returns an error result with message "Invalid admin code" and does not modify the caller's role.
The function integrates with the existing user profile storage — no new storage structures are introduced.
No existing backend functions, data types, or storage are modified.

requirements.text: Add a new frontend route `/admin/upgrade` to the existing TanStack Router configuration in `frontend/src/App.tsx`. This route must require only that the user is logged in (no role restriction). The page must render: a title "Admin Upgrade", a text input labelled "Enter Admin Setup Code", and a button labelled "Upgrade to Admin". On button click, call the backend `upgradeCurrentUserToAdmin` function with the entered code. On success, redirect the user to `/admin/dashboard`. On failure, display the error message returned by the backend. This route must NOT be linked or referenced anywhere in the UI — it is accessible by direct URL only.

quotes: Create a new route: /admin/upgrade — Accessible to any logged-in user
Page must contain: Title: "Admin Upgrade" — Input field: "Enter Admin Setup Code" — Button: "Upgrade to Admin"
The /admin/upgrade page should be hidden (direct URL only). Do not add buttons/links anywhere else.

acceptanceCriteria: Navigating directly to `/admin/upgrade` while logged in shows the Admin Upgrade page with the title, input field, and button.
Unauthenticated users navigating to `/admin/upgrade` are redirected to the landing page (consistent with existing auth guard behavior).
Entering code NAMMA5600 and clicking "Upgrade to Admin" calls the backend function, and on success redirects to `/admin/dashboard`.
Entering an incorrect code displays the error message "Invalid admin code" on the page.
No link, button, or reference to `/admin/upgrade` is added anywhere in the existing UI (Navigation, LandingPage, dashboards, modals, etc.).
The existing `/admin/dashboard` route guard (requires role == admin) is not modified.
No other existing pages, components, or logic are changed.

constraints: Do NOT modify any existing pages: CustomerDashboard, DriverDashboard, AdminDashboard, LandingPage, RoleSelectionPage, or any other existing component.
Do NOT modify existing backend functions, data types, or storage structures.
Do NOT add any navigation links or buttons pointing to /admin/upgrade anywhere in the UI.
Do NOT modify the /admin/dashboard route guard — it must continue to require role == admin.
Do NOT scaffold a new app or rebuild from scratch — this is a minimal additive patch only.
Integrate with the existing role storage (same HashMap/store used by current role getter/checker).
Do NOT modify frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or any files under frontend/src/components/ui.
nonGoals: Rebuilding or scaffolding the app from scratch.
Modifying customer booking form, driver dashboard, trip logic, filtering logic, or accept logic.
Adding visible navigation links or UI entry points to the /admin/upgrade page.
Modifying the admin dashboard page or its route guard.
Creating new storage structures or changing existing data schemas.
Any migration changes (no schema changes are involved).
